entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A legume crop produced in West Africa facing productivity and quality challenges

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: A region in Africa where groundnut is produced, including Mali, Niger, Nigeria, and Senegal

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of crop output per unit of input, which is currently low for groundnut-based systems

entities.name: AFLATOXIN
entities.description: A toxic contaminant subject to regulations that affect the competitiveness of groundnut markets

entities.name: SEED SYSTEMS
entities.description: The framework for the production, distribution, and uptake of seeds by farmers

entities.name: SEED QUALITY
entities.description: The standard of seeds, where high quality is fundamental for transforming agricultural production

entities.name: TRADITIONAL AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTION PRACTICES
entities.description: Conventional farming methods that need transformation to achieve sustainable food production

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE FOOD PRODUCTION
entities.description: The goal of achieving stable and long-term food output in West Africa

entities.name: YIELD POTENTIAL
entities.description: The maximum yield a crop variety can achieve under optimal conditions

entities.name: TRADITIONAL VARIETIES
entities.description: Local groundnut varieties used by farmers, which may have constraints

entities.name: CFC
entities.description: An organization that provided support for germplasm conservation and variety maintenance

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: An international research organization supporting groundnut germplasm conservation and variety development

entities.name: GERMPLASM CONSERVATION
entities.description: The process of preserving genetic resources of groundnut

entities.name: VARIETY MAINTENANCE
entities.description: The practice of ensuring the genetic purity and availability of groundnut varieties

entities.name: SEED MULTIPLICATION AND DISTRIBUTION SCHEMES
entities.description: Programs designed to increase and spread the availability of improved seeds

entities.name: STATE SEED PROJECTS
entities.description: Government-led initiatives for seed production and distribution

entities.name: LIBERALIZATION OF ECONOMIES
entities.description: The reduction of government restrictions in economic activities, leading to disengagement from seed production

entities.name: STRUCTURAL ADJUSTMENT POLICIES
entities.description: Economic policies that have influenced government withdrawal from seed distribution

entities.name: SEED SUPPLY SYSTEMS
entities.description: The formal and informal networks that provide seeds to farmers

entities.name: MALI
entities.description: A West African country analyzed for its groundnut seed supply systems

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: A West African country analyzed for its groundnut seed supply systems

entities.name: NIGERIA
entities.description: A West African country analyzed for its groundnut seed supply systems

entities.name: SENEGAL
entities.description: A West African country analyzed for its groundnut seed supply systems

entities.name: BREEDER SEED
entities.description: The initial seed produced by breeders, whose low supply limits the seed industry

entities.name: SEED DEMAND ESTIMATION
entities.description: The process of predicting the amount of seed required by farmers

entities.name: NATIONAL VARIETY RELEASE COMMITTEES
entities.description: Bodies responsible for approving new crop varieties for official release

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL FEATURES
entities.description: The inherent biological characteristics of groundnut that affect seed industry performance

entities.name: PRIVATE SECTOR ENTRY
entities.description: The involvement of private businesses in the seed industry

entities.name: LOCAL VILLAGE SEED SCHEMES
entities.description: Community-level arrangements for seed production and distribution

entities.name: PRIVATE SEED ENTREPRENEURS
entities.description: Small-scale business individuals engaged in the seed industry

entities.name: COMMUNITY-BASED SEED SYSTEMS
entities.description: Seed systems managed by local communities to ensure seed availability

entities.name: VERTICAL INTEGRATION
entities.description: The coordination between different stages of the supply chain, from inputs to product markets

entities.name: SEED PRODUCERS
entities.description: Entities responsible for producing seeds for planting

entities.name: GRAIN PRODUCERS
entities.description: Farmers who produce groundnuts for consumption or sale as grain

entities.name: PROCESSORS
entities.description: Entities that process groundnut grain into final products

entities.name: SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: The broader region where agriculture-dependent economies seek improved seed markets
